reforms.description: In March 2003, the Italian parliament approved a comprehensive reform of the primary and secondary education system, which includes an increase in compulsory schooling from 10 to 12 years and the introduction of a new vocational track to improve the link between education and the labor market. Additionally, the government has initiated a training programme for public administration workers, establishing minimum quality standards for training schools and requiring quality certification.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby improving productivity and economic competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform was approved by the parliament and is in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in the education system.
reforms.source_quote: Each public administration is now obliged to present a training programme for its workers at the beginning of every year.

reforms.description: The Italian government presented a new Company Law aimed at improving the efficiency and competitiveness of the economy by fostering entrepreneurial activity and enhancing capital market funding. Additionally, an e-government programme is being implemented to improve public service delivery through digital means, including a unified electronic access point for services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms are designed to enhance the business environment and improve public service efficiency, which can lead to increased economic activity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The Company Law has been presented and is awaiting implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant changes in the legal and operational framework for businesses and public services.
reforms.source_quote: The government has presented a company law that will come into effect in 2004... its main objective is to improve the efficiency and competitiveness of the Italian economy by fostering entrepreneurial activity.

reforms.description: The Italian government designed new measures to promote the economic recovery of the Mezzogiorno, focusing on improving infrastructure, including upgrading water supply systems and modernising transport networks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving infrastructure is essential for economic recovery and can facilitate business operations and attract investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been designed and are in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The measures represent a significant investment in infrastructure aimed at regional economic recovery.
reforms.source_quote: The new pact sets out some objectives regarding the Mezzogiorno, one of the most important ones being the improvement of infrastructures.
